name: Shackleton's Endurance Expedition (1914–17)
description: A simplified route of Shackleton's Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition: departure, drift in pack ice, lifeboat journey, and rescue.

moments.title: Departure from South Georgia
moments.text: Endurance leaves South Georgia, heading towards the Weddell Sea and the Antarctic continent.

moments.title: Trapped in the Ice
moments.text: Endurance becomes stuck fast in pack ice, beginning a long drift across the Weddell Sea.

moments.title: Ship Crushed
moments.text: After months locked in ice, Endurance is crushed and sinks, forcing the crew onto the floes.

moments.title: Elephant Island
moments.text: The crew reaches bleak Elephant Island in open boats—safe from the ice but stranded.

moments.title: James Caird Landfall
moments.text: Shackleton and a small party reach South Georgia after a perilous open-boat voyage.

moments.title: Rescue
moments.text: The remaining men are rescued without loss of life, making the expedition a legendary survival story.

points.label: Endurance trapped in the Weddell Sea pack ice
Shackleton reaches South Georgia in the James Caird
